Polytechnique Montreal

Description

FSE 2026

The ACM International Conference on the Foundations of Software Engineering (FSE) is an internationally renowned forum for researchers, practitioners, and educators to present and discuss the most recent innovations, trends, experiences, and challenges in the field of software engineering. FSE brings together experts from academia and industry to exchange the latest research results and trends as well as their practical application in all areas of software engineering.

We have designed an five-day program designed to bring together international experts from academia and industry to foster innovation, share knowledge, and build lasting connections.

This year’s conference is packed with opportunities to learn, compete, network, and discover.

Our program includes:

4 Co-located Conferences (AIWARE, PROMISE, SecDev, SSBSE).

15 Workshops on emerging topics (autonomous systems, games, generative art, sustainability, Human-Centered AI for SE, and quantum software engineering).

New This Year: 4 FSE-AIWARE Joint Competitions.

New This Year: Re-routed Presentations from Past Years that accepts missed presentations from ICSE25, FSE25, ASE25, ICSE26
